title: Vignantha stricta
intro: Vigna stricta, also known as the Indian lablab or wild hyacinth, is a perennial legume plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Vigna stricta include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Vigna stricta is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or paste, and is also used to make infusions, teas, and topical applications.
taxonomy: Vignantha stricta is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Millettieae. The genus Vignantha is part of the subfamily Faboideae and is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Vignantha stricta is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification places it among the flowering plants.
